name: manhattan-generic
description: Statistical description of a generic dense Manhattan block. Tile = one 800x264 block plus its share of right-of-way: a 100-ft avenue (60-ft street + two 20-ft sidewalks) on one long side and a 60-ft cross street on one short side, giving a 900x324 tile. 5-10 buildings share ~90% lot coverage; stories roughly lognormal, 4 to 30, median 8. Each building has its own stairs and elevators.

solids.derivation: block 800x264 = 211,200 sf; 90% lot coverage = 190,080 sf shared across buildings
notes: light_fractions DECLARED, not derived: crude stand-in for ~100-ft-deep floorplates lit from two sides at 25-ft useful depth, degraded further because the 45-degree rule fails on lower floors across 60-ft streets facing 8-30 story neighbors. Replace with the daylight tool.

circulation_fraction 0.15 is a typical core+corridor+stair+elevator factor. NOTE: platonic gens 1-3 declare 0 (never charged in the spec), so this comparison is not apples-to-apples; it overstates the platonic advantage by roughly that margin.
